data.title: Strategy is acquiring 'quite a lot' of bitcoin, Saylor says, amid sell-off
data.description:
Michael Saylor said Friday that Strategy (MSTR), the bitcoin (BTC-USD) treasury giant and business analytics firm he co-founded, is buying "quite a lot" of the crypto rout intensifies.

"We're buying quite a lot," Saylor told CNBC in an interview, adding that Strategy (MSTR) will disclose its latest bitcoin (BTC-USD) purchases on Monday. He reassured that investors may be "pleasantly surprised," as "we've been accelerating our purchases."

The bitcoin (BTC-USD) evangelist's remarks come as BTC's price briefly fell to below $95K for the first time in about six months -- on the heels of sizable net outflows from spot bitcoin exchange-traded funds -- as a wave of risk aversion swept through global markets.

"We're always buying," Saylor told CNBC, underscoring his unwavering long-term confidence in BTC. "Bitcoin is always a good investment," but only if "you can handle the volatility" with long time horizons of at least four years.

As of Nov. 9, 2025, Strategy (MSTR) held 641,692 bitcoin (BTC-USD), valued at roughly $61B at current prices. MSTR's aggregate stash has an average purchase price of ~$74.1K, compared with ~$96.2K at press time.

data.title: LM Funding America targets efficiency gains with 70 petahash expansion and share buyback amid treasury growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LM Funding America (LMFA) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Bruce Rodgers, Chairman, CEO & President, stated that the third quarter focused on "execution, integration and disciplined capital allocation, as we continue building LM Funding into a vertically integrated Bitcoin miner with a simple ambition: increase Bitcoin per share and grow intrinsic value over time." He highlighted the acquisition of an 11-megawatt facility in Columbus, Mississippi, expanding total capacity to 26 megawatts and diversifying operational risk. Rodgers reported, "By the end of September, we had integrated Mississippi, energized additional capacity and exited the month with approximately 304.5 Bitcoin in treasury, valued in at nearly $35 million versus a market capitalization of roughly half that amount."
  • Rodgers described a private securities repurchase that "retired more than 3.3 million shares and over 7.3 million warrants in a single transaction, reducing dilution, simplifying our capital structure and increasing our Bitcoin per share." He also announced a $1.5 million stock buyback and the deployment of Bitmain S21 immersion-cooled machines to further improve efficiency.
  • Ryan Duran, President of US Digital & Mining Co and Vice President of Operations, noted a "50% hash rate expansion in build cycle," with Mississippi adding 7.5 megawatts of energized capacity at a power cost of $0.036 per kilowatt hour. Duran said, "Bitcoin production increased 28% sequentially, rising from 5.9 Bitcoin in September to 7.6 Bitcoin in October."
  • Richard Russell, Chief Financial Officer, reported, "For the third quarter, revenue was $2.2 million, up approximately 13% sequentially and 74% year-over-year." Russell added, "Mining margins improved to 49%, driven by a shift from hosting fees to self-mining, utilizing our curtailment and energy sales to offset mining expenses and higher fleet efficiency." He said, "We reported a net loss of $3.7 million and a core EBITDA loss of $1.4 million, both driven by increased staff costs and payroll expenses."

Outlook

  • Management emphasized continuing the strategy to "balance Bitcoin accumulation, strategic investment and opportunistic share repurchases that we'll use only when it strengthens the balance sheet without sacrificing per share value." Rodgers reiterated, "Every action we take, every machine deployment, every site decision, every capital move is designed to improve per share ownership, per share cash flow and per share Bitcoin."
  • Duran projected that new Bitmain S21 immersion-cooled machines will "add roughly 70 petahash of compute power" and are scheduled to be energized in December, with expectations for a "step change in efficiency and should meaningfully increase Bitcoin per megawatt at the site."

Financial Results

  • Russell stated, "For the third quarter, revenue was $2.2 million, up approximately 13% sequentially and 74% year-over-year." Mining margins improved to 49%. Curtailment and energy sales totaled $152,000, down from $223,000 in Q2. The company reported a net loss of $3.7 million and a core EBITDA loss of $1.4 million, citing increased staff and payroll expenses as key drivers.
  • At quarter end, LM Funding held $300,000 in cash and cash equivalents and 304 Bitcoin valued at $34.7 million. As of October 31, the Bitcoin treasury stood at approximately 295 Bitcoin valued at $31.9 million or $2.62 per share.
  • Russell stated, "Our liquidity, treasury and credit capacity give us flexibility to support operations, growth and continued share repurchases while limiting dilution and preserving long-term upside for shareholders."

Q&A

  • Matthew Galinko, Maxim Group LLC: "With your mining infrastructure pretty radically different from where it was entering '25, I'm curious if you could maybe give us some thoughts on how you think about that your path in '26 as far as the Bitcoin mining infrastructure and equipment goes?" Rodgers replied, "The Mississippi acquisition has worked really, really well... we've got a nice runway there that we didn't anticipate. So I look for more growth there and on the magnitude of what we've achieved this year, it seems foreseeable."
  • Galinko followed up: "...you're not necessarily pursuing or close on any additional site acquisitions or is that something you're still exploring...?" Rodgers said, "We always have people exploring site acquisitions when we do it based on where the energy tariffs and then we look for a property that goes with those energy tariffs."
  • Galinko asked about capital allocation between mining and direct Bitcoin acquisition. Rodgers explained the company plays a long game, "looking at what do we think the price of Bitcoin is. And that means you don't necessarily make a dollar decision based on the current circumstances."
  • Sky Moore, for Kevin Dede, HCW: "...with about 15% of your old machines in storage... how are you guys managing your fleet... going forward?" Duran replied, "...having those machines in the wings is a great way to quickly deploy once that power becomes available."
  • Moore requested the current cost of mining one Bitcoin. Russell answered, "Our current mining costs right now per Bitcoin for this most recent quarter was $66,000. Last quarter, it was like $70,000."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a positive to neutral tone, commending operational progress and raising questions on future growth, site acquisition, capital allocation, and cost structure.
  • Management adopted a confident and forward-looking tone, emphasizing operational achievements, capital discipline, and efficiency gains. Phrases such as "we are entering our next efficiency phase" and "every action we take... is designed to improve per share ownership" reflected conviction.
  • The tone was more constructive and detailed this quarter, with management providing deeper explanations and transparency around capital allocation, operational efficiency, and growth levers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from focusing on M&A and immersion expansion in Q2 to emphasizing integration, efficiency, and per share economics in Q3.
  • Strategic focus moved from asset acquisition to capital structure optimization, share buybacks, and maximizing Bitcoin per share.
  • Analyst questions evolved from clarifications on asset deployment (Q2) to exploring the long-term capital allocation strategy and efficiency gains (Q3).
  • Key metric changes included sequential revenue growth, margin improvement, and a reduction in per Bitcoin mining costs from $70,000 to $66,000.
  • Management's tone was more assertive regarding treasury growth and operational leverage, while analysts maintained a constructive approach.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted the primary challenge remains in balancing capital allocation between mining expansion and outright Bitcoin purchases, as well as optimizing fleet deployment for maximum efficiency.
  • Analyst questions highlighted concerns about capital allocation, site acquisition opportunities, and cost containment in mining operations.
  • Russell highlighted staff and payroll expenses as drivers of the net loss, but emphasized the company's flexibility to support operations and growth through liquidity and credit capacity.

Final Takeaway

LM Funding America concentrated on scaling its vertically integrated mining operations, expanding capacity, and enhancing per share Bitcoin economics through disciplined capital allocation. The company executed a share and warrant repurchase, authorized a new buyback, and announced the addition of 70 petahash in immersion-cooled compute power. Management reported improved mining margins, lower per Bitcoin costs, and increased treasury value relative to market capitalization, underscoring a strategy focused on operational efficiency, shareholder alignment, and long-term Bitcoin accumulation, with an eye toward further efficiency and production gains in the coming quarters.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: WBD amends CEO Zaslav's contract as it explores sale, separation options
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) disclosed in an SEC filing on Thursday after the bell that it has amended the employment agreement for CEO David Zaslav amid the planned separation the company is pursuing and the strategic options it is currently exploring.

The filing showed that Zaslav has agreed to amend his tenure and nonqualified stock option agreements, originally entered on June 12, 2025.

The amendment clarifies that in the event the company enters a transaction that would result in a "change in control" before the end of 2026, Zaslav's employment contract will be extended until December 31, 2030.

The filing also said in the event the company pursues a separation or a reverse spinoff by the end of next year, Zaslav's employment contract will be extended until December 31, 2030.

"This extension is intended to secure Mr. Zaslav’s leadership of WBD for the same period that we had contracted to have him serve as the Chief Executive Officer of Warner Bros. following aSeparation," the filing said. "This ensures that, if the Strategic Review leads to our entering into a Qualifying CIC Agreement before the Outside Date, Mr. Zaslav will have the same opportunity to vest in, and incentives from, the Signing Options that he would have received had a Separation been completed in 2026."

Source: SEC filing

data.title: Short sellers boost bets against material stocks in October
data.description:
Bets against materials stocks edged up at the end of October compared to a month ago. International Paper (IP) took the spot for the most shorted stock in the sector, surpassing Albemarle (ALB), for the third consecutive month.

Average short interest across materials stocks in the S&P 500 index rose to 3.13% of the shares float as of Oct. 31, up from 2.85% at the end of September.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

 

 

International Paper (IP) had a short interest of 10.04% of the total float, followed by Albemarle (ALB), which had 10.08M shares sold short as of Oct. 31, or 8.63% of the shares float. CF Industries (CF) was the third most shorted materials stock at 7.21% of the shares float, compared to 5.83% at the end of September.

Water solutions company Ecolab (ECL), meanwhile, continues to hold the spot for the least shorted. Copper miner Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) 1.48%, DuPont de Nemours (DD) 1.62%, and Corteva (CTVA) 1.62%, were also among the list of least shorted securities at the end of October.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

 

Containers & packaging remained the most shorted industry within the sector, with 4.36% short interest as of October-end. Chemical stocks took the second spot with 3.02% short interest, compared to 2.54% a month ago.

Construction Materials was the least shorted sub-sector, with 4.36% short interest.

The S&P 500 Materials sector (NYSEARCA:XLB) has advanced 4.8% so far this year, largely underperforming the benchmark index S&P 500 (SP500), which has gained ~16% in the same period.

Here are some ETFs to track the materials sector: (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI)

data.title: Most shorted S&P 500 stocks in October
data.description:
Moderna (MRNA) retained its spot as the most shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float in October, followed by Charter Communications (CHTR) and The Campbell's Company (CPB).

Moderna shorts cut bets

Moderna (MRNA) had 17.62% of its shares sold short at October-end, down from 18.66% in September. The biotechnology company's shares have fallen nearly 40% year-to-date, largely underperforming the broader market and health care sector fund (XLV), which has advanced 11% in the same period. The company’s recent Q3 report showed a continued decline in COVID vaccine sales.

Charter Communications shorts raise bets

Charter Communications (CHTR) had 17.62% of its shares sold short as of October, up from 15.61% in the prior month. The stock has seen a string of analyst downgrades recently, following its third-quarter results. KeyBanc Capital Markets downgraded Charter (CHTR) to "Sector Weight" from "Overweight,” citing weak broadband subscriber growth. CHTR shares are down 40% year-to-date.

Campbell's Company shares under pressure, growth outlook weakens

Campbell's Company (CPB) took the third spot, with 16.1% of its shares sold short as of October. The packaged food firm has seen its shares fall 25.4% year-to-date. “Campbell’s Company remains a hold, as operational progress is offset by an uncertain near-term growth and earnings outlook. FY2026 guidance projects flat-to-negative organic growth and declining EPS, with margin recovery hinging on successful cost savings and marketing investments,” Seeking Alpha analyst Value Sights said.

Here are the top 20 most shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of October:

  • Moderna (MRNA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 18.21%
  • Charter Communications (CHTR) - Short Interest as a % of float: 17.62%

  • Omnicom Group  (OMC) - Short Interest as a % of float: 14.59%
  • Brown-Forman (BF.B) - Short Interest as a % of float: 12.7%
  • Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) - Short Interest as a % of float: 12.65%
  • MGM Resorts International (MGM) - Short Interest as a % of float: 11.19%
  • Molson Coors Beverage Company (TAP) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.95%

data.title: Applied Materials dips on soft near-term growth, but long-term position remains stout: analysts
data.description:
Applied Materials (AMAT) had fallen 7% by early trading on Friday due to weak near-term growth revealed in its latest financial results, but analysts remain positive on its long-term position as revenue is slated to begin accelerating in the second half of 2026.

One upside for Applied Materials stems from the U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security recently suspending an export rule affecting Applied Materials' exports to China for one year.

"Applied previously guided for the new U.S. BIS rules to reduce F4Q revenue by $110M and FY26 by $600M (roughly $150M/Q)," said Stifel analysts in a Friday investor note. "Earlier this week, the U.S. suspended those rules (for 1-yr, as a concession for bi-lateral trade negotiations with China), and Applied now expects the $110M that did not ship in F4Q to revenue in F1Q."

Stifel maintained its Buy rating and $250 price target following the financial results and outlook.

"While China is still expected to be a headwind in FY26, Applied was the first semicap to call for and see a slowdown in China," Stifel added. "Especially given the about-face on the prior China restrictions, we are optimistic that quarterly China sales could stabilize sooner rather than later."

Similarly, Needham reiterated its Buy rating and $260 price target on the stock.

"Management noted that customer visibility has significantly improved vs. a quarter ago, and now expects CY26 to be a growth year, 2H weighted, with leading-edge foundry/logic the top grower, followed by DRAM, while China and ICAPS are expected to be down," said Needham analysts Charles Shi and Denis Pyatchanin in a note. "Management sees WFE mix has been unfavorable in '25, which has led to underperformance, but should become much more favorable in '26."

Meanwhile, KeyBanc maintained its Overweight rating and $240 price target.

"In short, AMAT is not indicating material systems growth until C2H26, with strength from DRAM and Leading-edge Logic offsetting digestion in ICAPS capacity adds, particularly in China, while the now more streamlined AGS segment returns to DD growth," said KeyBanc analysts, led by Steve Barger, in a note. "That said, we think AMAT sounded confident in its positioning to win in its targeted growth markets, and we remain positive on the relative play vs. peers YTD."

KeyBanc also nudged up its earnings per share estimate for the full year of 2026 to $9.72 from $9.67 and 2027 up to $10.91 from $10.86.

Finally, Bank of America reiterated its Buy rating and $250 price objective.

"AMAT shared that customer conversations suggested a sharp acceleration in WFE in C2H’26 (so FQ4’26/FQ1’27), but that C1H is flattish HoH with C2H’25," said BofA analysts, led by Vivek Arya, in a note. "While this implies that FY26 will see limited sales growth, this is also the same linearity that peers LRCX & KLAC are observing next year. Further, with China now normalizing to 29% of sales, we think mix should begin to favor AMAT as the upcoming ramp should be skewed towards DRAM (leading WFE share) and leading-edge foundry/logic (on track to capture >50% of SAM). So, after being left behind in CY25, we are still optimistic that AMAT should return to WFE+ growth in the medium-term."

Applied Materials' competitors Lam Research (LRCX) and ASML Holding (ASML) were both down about 3% on Friday morning.

data.title: Stifel moves to the sidelines on Home Depot and Lowe's ahead of seasonal slowdown, delayed recovery
data.description:
As the home improvement sector enters a seasonal slowdown and faced with indications that the category is deteriorating from last year, Stifel downgraded Home Depot (HD) to Hold from Buy and trimmed 16% from its price target to reflect a weaker second half to 2025 and delayed recovery in the home improvement space.

“We continue to believe the drivers of the home improvement category are firmly in-tact, but believe the near-term setup is too challenging to maintain our positive approach,” Stifel’s W. Andrew Carter said in a note to clients.

Along with his downgrade to Home Depot (HD), Carter also sees Hold-rated Lowe’s (LOW) suspending any share repurchases until the second half of 2027 and meaningfully lowers his comparable sales estimate for both Lowe’s (LOW) and Home Depot (HD) ahead of third quarter results next week.

Notably, Carter does not believe Wall Street fully appreciates the magnitude of these revisions and expects Home Depot’s (HD) third quarter results and commentary will “illuminate a weaker SRS Distribution performance relative to initial expectations with the shortfall driving scrutiny for Home Depot’s Complex Pro efforts,” Carter adds.

Ahead of Q3 results, Carter now expects Home Depot (HD) will report comparable sales of +0.7% versus earlier estimates of +1.9% and EPS of $3.73 from $3.85, initially. Fourth quarter estimates have also been lowered to reflect softer comparable store sales and now expects Home Depot (HD) will have to adjust current guidance to reflect the lackluster comp sales performance anticipated in FY25.

For Lowe’s (LOW), Carter anticipates Q3 comparable sales to contract by 0.4% from +1.3% prior, and EPS of $2.89 from $2.94. For Q4, comparable sales are expected to again contract by 0.4% from +0.9%, and EPS revised down to $2.07 from $2.18.

Home Depot (HD) reports before the market open on Tuesday, November 18, expected to have earned a profit of $3.84 per share on $41.14B in sales.

Lowe’s (LOW) reports before the open on Wednesday, November 19, and expects to have earned a profit of $2.98 per share on $20.89B in sales.
